repo_notes.content: SCOPE: Generate EXACTLY three or four English-only pages: 'Skill Mapping', 'Technical Evidence', 'Quantifiables', and conditionally 'Dependency Graph' (required for repos with inter-process communication; omitted for single-process libraries — see Dependency Graph page notes). Zero Chinese text. All pages serve as human-readable documentation AND as structured input for an automated extraction pipeline. Do NOT generate resume bullets, accomplishment statements, or any text intended for direct inclusion in a resume. Your job is EVIDENCE EXTRACTION, not resume writing.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: TAXONOMY FILE: The file .devin/taxonomy.yaml in this repo defines 8 tracks (T1-T8), each with 5 levels (L0-L4). Each level contains a 'skills' map of snake_case skill keys, each with an 'aliases' array. When mapping technologies to skills, use ONLY (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pairs that exist in this file. Format: taxonomy_ref = 'T{1-8}-L{0-4}', skill_name = a key under that level's skills map. If a technology has no match, list it separately as an unmapped skill with a suggested track and level.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: STRUCTURED DATA PREFERENCE: Each page MUST contain ONE primary fenced YAML code block (```yaml ... ```) as its structured payload. All supplementary discussion should be OUTSIDE the code block as prose. The YAML block must be parseable by a strict YAML parser — no trailing commas, no unquoted special characters, no tab indentation. If a string contains colons or special chars, wrap in single quotes.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: EVIDENCE RULES: Every claim must cite at least one source file path and approximate line range. Format: {file: 'path/to/file', lines: '42-87', summary: '≤20 words'}. Classify evidence as: 'implemented' (actual usage in source code — function definitions, algorithm implementations, API calls), 'configured' (launch files, config files, parameter tuning), 'declared' (package.json, CMakeLists.txt, requirements.txt dependency only), or 'tested' (appears in test files). Only 'implemented' and 'configured' count as proficiency evidence. 'declared' is noted but flagged. If line numbers are uncertain, prefix with '~'. If path is inferred, append '[inferred]'.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: ATS KEYWORD SOURCING: The 'aliases' arrays in .devin/taxonomy.yaml are the PRIMARY source for exact ATS keywords. Separate keywords into: 'exact' (drawn verbatim from taxonomy aliases) and 'semantic' (additional terms observed in code — class names, variable names, API calls, ROS topic names, message types — that are NOT already in the aliases array). Do NOT duplicate taxonomy aliases into semantic. Semantic keywords must include the source_file where they were observed.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: QUANTIFICATION RULES: When extracting numbers, always include: (1) the raw value, (2) the unit, (3) the source file/line, (4) whether the number is 'measured' (from benchmarks, tests, logs), 'configured' (from config/params), 'counted' (from code structure like file count, test count), or 'claimed' (from README/docs with no code/test evidence — always tag '[unverified]'). Mark any derived or estimated values with '[est.]' and show the derivation. Prefer concrete numbers over vague qualifiers.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content:
USAGE DEPTH CALIBRATION: The usage_depth enum has strict definitions and imposes a CEILING on claimable taxonomy levels:
- 'declared': Appears only in dependency manifests (package.xml, requirements.txt, CMakeLists find_package). Ceiling: L0.
- 'imported': Import/include statements exist and the library is called, but only via default API with no customization. Ceiling: L1.
- 'configured': Parameters tuned, launch files customized, config files authored for this specific use case. Ceiling: L2.
- 'customized': Source-level modifications — subclassed, extended, wrote custom plugins, non-trivial algorithmic usage. Ceiling: L3.
- 'architected': Designed the system around this technology — defined interfaces, made architectural trade-offs, wrote abstractions others consume. Ceiling: L4.
If the taxonomy_ref level EXCEEDS the usage_depth ceiling, this is a FLAG — Stage 2 will reject the claim. When in doubt, classify DOWN, not up.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: CROSS-PAGE REFERENCES: Every skill in Skill Mapping MUST reference at least one evidence_unit_ref (from Technical Evidence) and SHOULD reference metric_refs (from Quantifiables) when applicable. Every evidence unit MUST reference at least one quantifiable_ref if its statement involves any magnitude, frequency, count, or performance claim. IDs must be consistent across pages — E01 in Skill Mapping must be the same E01 in Technical Evidence, M01 in Skill Mapping must be the same M01 in Quantifiables. Stage 2 will REJECT pages with dangling references or inconsistent IDs.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL: (1) If you cannot locate a file path with confidence, DO NOT GUESS — use '[not found]' as the path and add '[inferred]' tag, and set classification to 'declared'. Stage 2 will attempt to resolve. (2) If line numbers are uncertain beyond ±20 lines, use '~' prefix AND set a 'confidence: low' field. (3) If you are unsure whether usage is 'configured' vs 'customized', ALWAYS choose the LOWER depth. (4) NEVER round metrics to clean numbers (e.g., do not turn 47.3 into '~50'). Extract the EXACT value from source. (5) If a README claims performance numbers but no code/test evidence supports them, extract with source_type: 'claimed' and flag: '[unverified]'. (6) If the repo contains fewer than 5 extractable metrics, that is FINE — do not pad with estimated values. Quality over quantity.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: PROJECT OUTCOMES PROTOCOL [NEW — feeds G1 fix]: Every Technical Evidence page MUST include a project_outcomes section at the top level, immediately after the project block, parallel to evidence_units. This section captures the ACCOMPLISHED dimension of XYZ that no amount of implementation evidence can substitute. Sub-sections: publications (conference/journal/workshop/preprint/thesis), deployments (simulation/hardware/cloud/embedded), benchmark_results (comparative performance claims with baseline). Rules: (1) A publication at a named robotics or ML venue (IROS, ICRA, RSS, RA-L, CoRL, NeurIPS, CVPR, ECCV, etc.) is a Tier-1 bullet anchor — extract venue name and year even if the only source is a README sentence tagged source_type: claimed. (2) If the README states 'accepted at X' or 'published at X' with no DOI or link, use source_type: claimed and confidence: medium. (3) If a DOI, arXiv ID, or conference proceedings URL is present in the repo, use source_type: verified and confidence: high. (4) If the repo makes no publication, deployment, or benchmark claim anywhere, all three sub-sections must be empty lists — never invent an outcome. (5) benchmark_results entries sourced only from README prose use source_type: claimed and append '[unverified]' to the metric_name. (6) A MISSING project_outcomes section is a Stage 2 BLOCKER: the pipeline will restrict all bullets from this repo to L0-L1 and emit a validation warning.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: ACHIEVEMENT UNITS vs EVIDENCE UNITS [NEW — G1 fix]: The Technical Evidence page MUST contain two structurally distinct lists: evidence_units (existing) and achievement_units (new, top-level after evidence_units). evidence_units describe WHAT THE CODE DOES — factual, implementation-level, verifiable from source files. achievement_units describe WHAT WAS ACCOMPLISHED — outcome-level, XYZ-ready, drawn from publications, deployments, README claims, and benchmark results. The grammatical subject is the key distinction: 'The system initializes a 32×32×30 TSDF voxel grid on GPU' is an evidence_unit. 'Engineered a GPU-accelerated TSDF bin-packing algorithm accepted at IROS 2023, demonstrating compact placement of irregular 3D objects in simulation' is an achievement_unit. Rules: (1) Every achievement_unit MUST have a non-null xyz_seed.accomplished — this is the X-component Stage 2 copies verbatim into xyz.accomplished. (2) Every achievement_unit MUST reference at least one evidence_unit via xyz_seed.evidence_unit_refs — the referenced units provide the Z-component (by_doing). (3) Do NOT paste implementation facts into achievement statements; rephrase as outcomes. (4) If the repo has no publications, deployments, or explicit performance claims, derive ONE minimal achievement_unit from project.purpose, tag source_type: inferred, and set confidence: low — this is acceptable and prevents the Stage 2 blocker. (5) A{nn} IDs are sequential in a namespace separate from E{nn} and M{nn}. (6) A page with zero achievement_units causes Stage 2 to produce no bullets above L1 for the entire repo — this is a hard ceiling, not a soft degradation.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content:
ACTION VERB CANDIDATES [NEW — G2 fix]: Every skill entry in Skill Mapping and every achievement_unit in Technical Evidence MUST include an action_verb_candidates list (1-3 verbs). Select from the four-tier hierarchy matched to usage_depth and evidence character:
- Tier 1 (usage_depth=architected, or novel algorithm evidence): Architected, Designed, Engineered, Formulated, Devised
- Tier 2 (usage_depth=customized|configured, integration/implementation work): Implemented, Built, Integrated, Extended, Customized, Adapted
- Tier 3 (optimization/measurement focus, regardless of depth): Optimized, Tuned, Accelerated, Profiled, Reduced, Improved
- Tier 4 (usage_depth=imported|declared, or evaluation-only): Evaluated, Benchmarked, Validated, Applied, Utilized
Rules: (1) Never assign Tier-1 verbs to usage_depth=imported or declared entries. (2) If the evidence describes a NOVEL ALGORITHM (not merely library usage), include at least one Tier-1 verb regardless of the overall usage_depth classification. (3) List the strongest candidate FIRST — Stage 2 reads index 0 as the default and uses alternatives only when the default creates a duplicate verb in the same resume section. (4) Add action_verb_candidates to each skill's top level, to each achievement_unit.xyz_seed, and to each evidence_unit.xyz_seed. (5) Missing verb candidates collapse all bullets to 'Developed', triggering ATS anti-stuffing rules at the 3× repetition cap.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: RESUME ANCHOR IDENTIFICATION [NEW — G3 fix]: After extracting all metrics in the Quantifiables page, add a resume_anchors top-level section (after derived_metrics) containing 3-7 entries. Each anchor synthesizes a high-potential metric combination into a draft bullet clause so Stage 2 performs a lookup rather than an inference. Required fields: metric_ids (1-3 M{nn}s that function together in one clause), anchor_type (quantitative_achievement | scale_signal | configuration_depth | publication_proxy), bullet_position (measured_by | accomplished | context), bullet_clause_template (draft clause using [value] and [unit] placeholders showing the metric's exact grammatical role in a bullet sentence), paired_evidence_units (E{nn} IDs whose by_doing_fragment provides the Z-clause for this anchor), and action_verb_candidate. Rules: (1) Every metric with resume_potential: high MUST appear in at least one resume_anchor — inconsistency is a Stage 2 validation error. (2) Physics simulator defaults (friction coefficients, gravity constant, ERP/CFM values) are ALWAYS bullet_position_hint: none and resume_potential: low — never anchor bullets on simulation engine configuration. (3) Frequency/rate metrics (Hz) are bullet_position_hint: measured_by only if above the domain-standard threshold for the task type (e.g., >100Hz for manipulation control). (4) Scale metrics (grid dimensions, dataset size, object count) are bullet_position_hint: context by default — upgrade to measured_by only if explicitly compared to a baseline. (5) A missing resume_anchors section causes Stage 2 to scan all metrics indiscriminately, producing noisy measured_by clauses.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: XYZ SEED ANNOTATION PROTOCOL [NEW — G4 fix, cross-page consistency]: Every evidence_unit and every achievement_unit MUST include a complete xyz_seed block. These are the primary join fields Stage 2 uses to assemble xyz{accomplished, measured_by, by_doing}. Schema for evidence_unit.xyz_seed: {xyz_role: <accomplished|by_doing|context|measured_by>, accomplished_fragment: '<X text — non-null ONLY when xyz_role=accomplished, null otherwise>', by_doing_fragment: '<Z text — ALWAYS required, never null, even for context units>', measured_by_metric_ids: ['M{nn}'], action_verb_candidate: '<Tier-1/2/3/4 verb>'}. Schema for achievement_unit.xyz_seed: {accomplished: '<X text — NEVER null>', measured_by_metric_ids: ['M{nn}'], evidence_unit_refs: ['E{nn}'], action_verb_candidate: '<Tier-1/2 verb>'}. Cross-page consistency rules enforced by Stage 2 validator BEFORE bullet assembly: (A) Every M{nn} in any xyz_seed.measured_by_metric_ids MUST exist in the Quantifiables page — dangling metric ref → REJECT. (B) Every E{nn} in achievement_unit.xyz_seed.evidence_unit_refs MUST exist in the evidence_units list — dangling evidence ref → REJECT. (C) Every A{nn} in cross_skill_bullet_seeds.achievement_unit_ref MUST exist in the achievement_units list — dangling seed ref → REJECT. (D) An evidence_unit with xyz_role: accomplished MUST have a non-null accomplished_fragment — null accomplished_fragment with accomplished role → REJECT. (E) Every evidence_unit MUST have a non-null by_doing_fragment regardless of xyz_role — context units have a 'by using' or 'by maintaining' interpretation that must be made explicit.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

pages.title: Skill Mapping
pages.purpose: Identify ALL technologies, frameworks, libraries, algorithms, and design patterns in this codebase. Map each to the robotics skill taxonomy in .devin/taxonomy.yaml using (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pairs. For each skill, assess usage depth (with ceiling check), provide file-level evidence, extract ATS keywords, provide action_verb_candidates (see repo note 12), and document typed relationships to other skills in the map. Also document the tech stack (languages with approximate LOC%, frameworks, tools). List technologies not covered by the taxonomy as unmapped skills. After the skills list, include a cross_skill_bullet_seeds section (see repo note G5 fix) that pre-computes multi-skill XYZ seeds for Stage 2 bullet synthesis.

page_notes.content:
PREFERRED YAML STRUCTURE:
```
schema_version: 3
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
generated_at: '<ISO-8601>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>', ...]
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']
skills:
  - taxonomy_ref: 'T{n}-L{n}'
    skill_name: '<snake_case from taxonomy>'
    usage_depth: <declared|imported|configured|customized|architected>
    usage_depth_rationale:
      classification_trigger: '<what specific code pattern justifies this depth>'
      ceiling_check: '<T{n}-L{n} vs depth ceiling → PASS|FAIL>'
    strongest_evidence_id: '<E{nn}>'
    file_count: <int>
    proficiency_evidence:
      - file: '<path>'
        lines: '<start-end>'
        classification: <implemented|configured|declared|tested>
        summary: '<≤30 words>'
        confidence: <high|medium|low>
    ats_keywords:
      exact: ['<from taxonomy aliases>']
      semantic:
        - keyword: '<observed term>'
          source_file: '<path where observed>'
    action_verb_candidates:
      - verb: '<Tier-1/2/3/4 verb>'
        tier: <1|2|3|4>
        rationale: '<≤15 words: why this verb fits usage_depth and evidence type>'
    bullet_assembly_hint:
      primary_bullet_type: <single_skill|co_implementation|pipeline_stage|system_design>
      recommended_xyz_anchor: '<A{nn} or E{nn} — the strongest X-component source for this skill>'
    related_skills:
      - target: '<taxonomy_ref:skill_name>'
        relationship_type: <co_implementation|pipeline_stage|dependency|interface_boundary|runtime_composition>
        integration_evidence:
          file: '<path>'
          lines: '<range>'
          summary: '<≤30 words showing the two skills interacting>'
        integration_pattern: '<≤30 words: how these two skills interact architecturally>'
        co_occurrence_files: <int>
    evidence_unit_refs: ['<E{nn}>', ...]
    metric_refs: ['<M{nn}>', ...]
tech_stack:
  languages:
    - name: '<lang>'
      version: '<if detectable>'
      loc_pct: <float>
  frameworks: [{name, version, role}]
  build_tools: [{name, version}]
  testing: [{framework, test_count_est}]
unmapped_skills:
  - name: '<technology>'
    observed_in: '<file>'
    suggested_track: 'T{n}'
    suggested_level: 'L{n}'
    rationale: '<why this track/level>'
cross_skill_bullet_seeds:
  - seed_id: 'BS{nn}'
    skills_involved: ['<T{n}-L{n}:skill_name>', ...]
    relationship_types: ['<co_implementation|pipeline_stage|dependency|...>']
    achievement_unit_ref: 'A{nn}'
    evidence_unit_refs: ['<E{nn}>', ...]
    metric_refs: ['<M{nn}>', ...]
    action_verb: '<recommended Tier-1 or Tier-2 verb>'
    xyz_seed:
      accomplished: '<X fragment: what the skill combination achieved — drawn from achievement_unit>'
      measured_by_metric_ids: ['<M{nn}>']
      by_doing: '<Z fragment: how the listed skills interact to produce the outcome — ≤40 words>'
    bullet_template_en: '<draft structure using [M{nn}] as placeholders for metric values — NOT a polished bullet>'
    taxonomy_refs_in_scope: ['<T{n}-L{n}>', ...]
    confidence: <high|medium|low>
```
After the YAML block, add prose sections: '## Architecture Context' (how components fit together) and '## Skill Relationship Graph' (which skills are used together, what the integration pattern is, and which files demonstrate the coupling). If this repo is a single-process library with no IPC, add the note: 'Dependency Graph page omitted: single-process library with no IPC architecture.'

RELATIONSHIP QUALITY: related_skills edges are the PRIMARY input for cross-track resume bullets. A flat list of taxonomy_refs is INSUFFICIENT. Each relationship MUST include: (1) the relationship_type enum value, (2) at least one file-level citation showing the two skills interacting in the SAME code region (not just the same repo), (3) a ≤25-word integration_pattern describing the architectural coupling. Relationship types map to bullet clause structures: co_implementation → 'Implemented X with Y', pipeline_stage → 'X feeding into Y', dependency → 'X built on Y', interface_boundary → 'X communicating with Y via Z', runtime_composition → 'X orchestrating Y at runtime'. If two skills appear in the same repo but never interact in the same file or call chain, they are NOT related — do not list them.

ACTION VERB CANDIDATES PER SKILL [NEW]: The action_verb_candidates field on each skill entry is the verb prior for every single-skill bullet generated from that skill entry. Tier selection rules keyed to usage_depth: architected → list one Tier-1 verb first; customized → Tier-2 first with optional Tier-1 if a novel algorithm is evident; configured → Tier-2 or Tier-3; imported → Tier-2 or Tier-4; declared → Tier-4 only. For any skill where the strongest_evidence_id points to a published novel algorithm, always include one Tier-1 verb regardless of usage_depth ceiling. The rationale field (≤15 words) must explain the verb-depth mapping — it is read by the Stage 2 validator to confirm the tier selection is consistent with usage_depth. Index 0 is the Stage 2 default; remaining candidates are used only to avoid duplicate verbs across bullets in the same resume section. Missing action_verb_candidates causes Stage 2 to emit 'Developed' for every bullet from this skill, collapsing diversity and triggering the 3× ATS anti-stuffing repetition cap.

CROSS-SKILL BULLET SEEDS [NEW — G5 fix]: The cross_skill_bullet_seeds section is the PRIMARY input for Stage 2's multi-skill bullet synthesis. Each seed pre-computes the XYZ frame for a skill combination so Stage 2 performs a structured lookup rather than runtime inference. Requirements: (1) Each seed MUST reference ≥2 skills from the skills list above — single-skill bullets are handled per-skill, not in this section. (2) achievement_unit_ref MUST reference a valid A{nn} from the Technical Evidence page — a seed without an accomplishment anchor produces no bullets and fails Stage 2 validation. (3) xyz_seed.accomplished is an X fragment, not the full X sentence — Stage 3 may expand it with ATS keywords before final rendering. (4) bullet_template_en uses [M{nn}] as placeholders for metric values that Stage 3 fills from Quantifiables at render time; write it as a grammatically complete sentence with placeholders, not a note about structure. (5) confidence: high requires all referenced A{nn}, E{nn}, and M{nn} IDs to be verified as non-dangling against their respective pages before the page is emitted. (6) If no multi-skill integration points exist in the repo, emit cross_skill_bullet_seeds: [] — the section must be present even as an empty list; omitting it entirely causes Stage 2 to skip multi-skill synthesis for the whole repo.

pages.title: Technical Evidence
pages.purpose: Document the architectural decisions, design patterns, algorithms, integration patterns, and engineering challenges evident in this codebase. Do NOT write resume bullets. Instead, extract three distinct structured artifacts: (1) evidence_units — atomic, factual statements about what the code does, each annotated with xyz_seed role assignments; (2) achievement_units — outcome-level accomplishment statements that form the X-component of XYZ bullets, drawn from publications, deployments, and benchmark claims; (3) project_outcomes — typed records of publications, deployments, and benchmark results. Group evidence units by the taxonomy tracks they relate to. Also extract the project's purpose, problem domain, and deployment context.

page_notes.content:
PREFERRED YAML STRUCTURE:
```
schema_version: 3
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>', ...]
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']
project:
  title: '<descriptive project name>'
  domain: '<e.g. mobile manipulation, autonomous navigation>'
  purpose: '<2-3 sentences: what problem does this solve>'
  deployment_context: '<simulation|hardware|both, what platform>'
project_outcomes:
  publications:
    - id: 'PUB{nn}'
      type: <conference|journal|workshop|preprint|thesis>
      venue: '<full venue name, e.g. IEEE/RSJ IROS 2023>'
      year: <int>
      title: '<paper title if stated anywhere in repo>'
      source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
      source_type: <claimed|verified>
      confidence: <high|medium|low>
  deployments:
    - id: 'DEP{nn}'
      context: <simulation|hardware|cloud|embedded>
      scale: '<brief description of what was deployed and at what scale>'
      source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
      confidence: <high|medium|low>
  benchmark_results:
    - id: 'BNR{nn}'
      metric_name: '<what was measured — append [unverified] if README-only>'
      value: <number>
      unit: '<unit>'
      baseline: '<what it is compared to, if stated>'
      source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
      source_type: <claimed|measured>
      confidence: <high|medium|low>
evidence_units:
  - id: 'E{nn}'
    category: <architecture|algorithm|integration|optimization|testing|deployment>
    statement: '<1-2 factual sentences about what the code does — NOT a resume bullet>'
    taxonomy_refs: ['<T{n}-L{n}:skill_name>']
    source:
      - file: '<path>'
        lines: '<range>'
        classification: <implemented|configured|declared|tested>
        summary: '<≤30 words>'
        confidence: <high|medium|low>
    complexity_indicators:
      - type: <algorithmic|systems|scale|realtime|safety>
        detail: '<what makes this non-trivial>'
    integration_context: '<what other components/skills does this connect to>'
    quantifiable_refs: ['<M{nn}>', ...]
    skill_refs: ['<T{n}-L{n}:skill_name>', ...]
    xyz_seed:
      xyz_role: <accomplished|by_doing|context|measured_by>
      accomplished_fragment: '<X text — non-null ONLY when xyz_role=accomplished, null otherwise>'
      by_doing_fragment: '<Z text — ALWAYS required, never null>'
      measured_by_metric_ids: ['<M{nn}>']
      action_verb_candidate: '<Tier-1/2/3/4 verb>'
    resume_potential: <high|medium|low>
    resume_potential_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
achievement_units:
  - id: 'A{nn}'
    category: <publication|deployment|scale|performance|novelty>
    statement: '<outcome-level accomplishment statement — NOT an implementation fact>'
    source:
      - {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>', classification: <claimed|verified|inferred>}
    taxonomy_refs: ['<T{n}-L{n}:skill_name>']
    xyz_seed:
      accomplished: '<X: what was achieved — NEVER null>'
      measured_by_metric_ids: ['<M{nn}>']
      evidence_unit_refs: ['<E{nn}>']
      action_verb_candidate: '<Tier-1 or Tier-2 verb>'
    confidence: <high|medium|low>
    confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
architectural_decisions:
  - decision: '<what was decided>'
    alternatives_visible: '<evidence of other approaches considered, if any>'
    evidence_file: '<path>'
    related_evidence_units: ['<E{nn}>', ...]
```
After the YAML block, add: '## Design Pattern Analysis' and '## Engineering Challenges' (inferred from code complexity, error handling, edge cases). These prose sections give the downstream pipeline context for composing XYZ-decomposed bullets.

EVIDENCE UNIT QUALITY: (1) Statements must be FACTUAL and VERIFIABLE from the cited files — no speculation about performance or impact. (2) Each evidence unit should be ATOMIC — one architectural fact, not a compound sentence. (3) Aim for 10-20 evidence units per repo (more for larger repos, but do NOT pad with trivial units). (4) Complexity indicators are critical: the downstream pipeline uses these to assess which evidence units warrant L3-L4 skill claims vs L1-L2. (5) IDs sequential: E01-E99. (6) Every evidence unit with ANY quantitative claim MUST have a quantifiable_refs link to the Quantifiables page — if the number is not extracted there, it cannot appear here. (7) Every evidence_unit MUST have a complete xyz_seed block — units without xyz_seed are REJECTED by the Stage 2 validator before bullet assembly begins. (8) by_doing_fragment is ALWAYS required and never null regardless of xyz_role — even a unit with xyz_role: context has a 'by using' or 'by maintaining' interpretation that must be made explicit so Stage 2 can compose complete Z-clauses.

ACHIEVEMENT UNITS ARE MANDATORY [NEW]: Every Technical Evidence page MUST contain an achievement_units section with at least one entry. Achievement units are structurally and semantically distinct from evidence_units: they describe OUTCOMES, not implementations. The grammatical test: does the statement answer 'what did this project achieve?' rather than 'what does the code do?' If the repo has a publication at a named venue, it MUST appear as an achievement_unit with category: publication and the venue name in xyz_seed.accomplished. If the repo has no publications, deployments, or explicit performance claims, derive ONE minimal achievement_unit from project.purpose, use source_type: inferred, and set confidence: low — this is acceptable and prevents the Stage 2 L1 ceiling blocker. Required field invariants that Stage 2 validates before bullet assembly: xyz_seed.accomplished must be non-null; xyz_seed.evidence_unit_refs must list at least one E{nn}; taxonomy_refs must contain at least one valid (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pair from Skill Mapping. A{nn} IDs are in a separate namespace from E{nn} and M{nn} — never reuse an ID across namespaces.

PROJECT OUTCOMES ARE MANDATORY [NEW]: Every Technical Evidence page MUST contain a project_outcomes section immediately after the project block. Even when all three sub-sections (publications, deployments, benchmark_results) are empty lists, the section key must be present — its absence is structurally indistinguishable from a generation failure and causes Stage 2 to treat the repo as an undocumented utility library. Key extraction rules by sub-section: publications — extract venue and year from any location in the repo (README, paper.bib, CITATION.cff, comments) even if source_type is claimed; the venue string (e.g., 'IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems 2023') is the primary Tier-1 bullet signal that Stage 3 uses to select senior-level framing. benchmark_results — if a README contains a comparison table, extract each row as a separate BNR{nn} entry with the stated improvement value, the baseline name, and source_type: claimed plus '[unverified]'; these become the measured_by candidates for performance bullets if no log evidence exists. deployments — 'PyBullet simulation with Ravens framework' qualifies as a deployment entry with context: simulation; it provides bullet context even if not a production deployment.

pages.title: Quantifiables
pages.purpose: Extract ALL numbers, metrics, and quantifiable signals from this codebase. This includes: configured parameters (control gains, rates, tolerances), code-structure counts (files, functions, test cases, LOC per module), performance-related values (frequencies, latencies, buffer sizes), and any benchmark or test results visible in the repo. For each metric, assign a bullet_position_hint and resume_potential label. After extracting all raw metrics, synthesize a resume_anchors section identifying the 3-7 highest-potential metric combinations and their role in bullet construction. Do NOT interpret metrics — extract them with source evidence and label them; the downstream pipeline decides which become resume metrics.

page_notes.content:
PREFERRED YAML STRUCTURE:
```
schema_version: 3
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>', ...]
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']
metrics:
  - id: 'M{nn}'
    category: <performance|scale|reliability|complexity|configuration|test_coverage>
    raw_value: <number or range>
    unit: '<e.g. Hz, ms, %, count, DOF, lines>'
    source_type: <measured|configured|counted|estimated|claimed>
    source:
      file: '<path>'
      lines: '<range>'
      context: '<≤15 words: where this number appears>'
    confidence: <high|medium|low>
    taxonomy_refs: ['<T{n}-L{n}:skill_name if applicable>']
    evidence_unit_refs: ['<E{nn}>']
    resume_potential: <high|medium|low>
    resume_potential_rationale: '<1 sentence explaining why this metric is or is not bullet-worthy>'
    bullet_position_hint: <accomplished|measured_by|by_doing|context|none>
code_structure:
  total_loc: <int>
  modules:
    - name: '<directory or package>'
      loc: <int>
      file_count: <int>
      primary_language: '<lang>'
  test_file_count: <int>
  dependency_count: <int>
  ros_nodes: <int, if applicable>
  ros_topics: <int, if applicable>
  launch_configurations: <int, if applicable>
derived_metrics:
  - id: 'D{nn}'
    description: '<what was calculated>'
    value: <number>
    unit: '<unit>'
    derivation: '<formula or logic used>'
    source_metrics: ['<M{nn}>', ...]
    confidence: <high|medium|low>
resume_anchors:
  - id: 'RA{nn}'
    metric_ids: ['<M{nn}>', ...]
    anchor_type: <quantitative_achievement|scale_signal|configuration_depth|publication_proxy>
    bullet_position: <measured_by|accomplished|context>
    bullet_clause_template: '<draft clause as a complete English phrase using [value] and [unit] placeholders>'
    paired_evidence_units: ['<E{nn}>']
    action_verb_candidate: '<Tier-1/2/3 verb>'
    confidence: <high|medium|low>
```
After the YAML block, add: '## Metric Interpretation Notes' (domain context for what these numbers mean in robotics — e.g., '480Hz physics simulation rate is a typical PyBullet real-time approximation, not a control-loop frequency') and '## Resume Anchor Rationale' (prose explaining why the top 3 anchors were selected and what bullet structure they support — this is read by Stage 2 to validate anchor intent).

EXTRACTION COMPLETENESS: Search these locations exhaustively: (1) Config/param files (YAML, JSON, XML, launch files) for configured values. (2) Constants and #define in source for hardcoded parameters. (3) Test files for expected values and tolerances. (4) README/docs for claimed performance — use source_type: 'claimed' and tag '[unverified]' if no code evidence. (5) CI/build configs for tool versions and flags. (6) ROS parameter servers, dynamic_reconfigure definitions. Every number must have a source citation. Aim for 15-40 metrics per repo, but if the repo yields fewer, do NOT invent metrics to reach the target.

RESUME ANCHORS ARE MANDATORY [NEW]: Every Quantifiables page MUST contain a resume_anchors section with 3-7 entries. Resume anchors are synthetic artifacts — they combine raw metrics into bullet-ready clause structures, which is the primary mechanism by which Quantifiables feeds the Stage 2 assembly join. Rules: (1) Every metric with resume_potential: high MUST appear in at least one resume_anchor as a member of metric_ids — a high-potential metric with no anchor entry is a Stage 2 validation error. (2) Physics simulator defaults (lateral_friction, rolling_friction, contact_cfm, contact_erp, gravity) are ALWAYS bullet_position_hint: none and resume_potential: low; never include them in any resume_anchor entry. (3) The bullet_clause_template must be a grammatically complete English phrase (not a note or description), for example: 'achieving physics-accurate simulation at [value] [unit] with [60] steps per object placement'. (4) paired_evidence_units links the anchor to the E{nn} units that provide the Z-clause when Stage 2 assembles the full bullet around this anchor. (5) anchor_type: publication_proxy is used when a metric (e.g., year of publication, acceptance rate of venue) derives its bullet value from a publication fact rather than a code measurement — these always have bullet_position: accomplished. (6) A missing resume_anchors section causes Stage 2 to scan all metrics indiscriminately and produce bullets where M01 (lateral_friction=0.5) appears as a measured achievement.

BULLET POSITION TAGGING [NEW]: Every metric MUST carry a bullet_position_hint. This is the primary filter Stage 2 applies before assembling measured_by clauses. Tagging guide by metric category: configuration (friction, gravity, ERP, CFM, mesh scale) → bullet_position_hint: none, resume_potential: low. performance/rate (Hz values above domain threshold, millisecond latencies) → bullet_position_hint: measured_by, resume_potential: high if above standard; medium if at standard; low if below. scale (grid dimensions, object counts, dataset sizes) → bullet_position_hint: context unless the scale is explicitly compared to a baseline, in which case measured_by. complexity (LOC, file count, function count) → bullet_position_hint: context unless LOC demonstrates exceptional engineering scope (>10K LOC single-module). test_coverage (%) → bullet_position_hint: measured_by if above industry standard for the domain (>80%). claimed performance improvement (%) → bullet_position_hint: accomplished with source_type: claimed and '[unverified]' appended. Tagging rule when uncertain: always default to context — it is never wrong and prevents bullet stuffing. The resume_potential_rationale field must explain the tagging decision in plain language so Stage 2 can emit a meaningful warning if the tag is overridden at runtime.

pages.title: Dependency Graph
pages.purpose: Extract the runtime communication architecture of this system. For ROS/ROS2 repos: map all nodes, topics (with message types), services, action servers, TF frames, and launch-file composition. For non-ROS repos: map all inter-process communication, API boundaries, event buses, shared-memory interfaces, and plugin architectures. This page captures SYSTEM-LEVEL DESIGN — how components are composed into a working pipeline. Do NOT interpret or evaluate the architecture; extract it factually with file citations. CONDITIONAL: If this repo has NO inter-process communication and is a single-process library, SKIP this page entirely and note in the Skill Mapping prose: 'Dependency Graph page omitted: single-process library with no IPC architecture.'

page_notes.content:
PREFERRED YAML STRUCTURE:
```
schema_version: 3
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>', ...]
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']
architecture_type: <ros2|ros1|microservice|monolith|plugin_based|hybrid>
nodes:
  - name: '<node_name>'
    source_file: '<path to main file>'
    lifecycle_managed: <true|false>
    publishes:
      - topic: '<topic_name>'
        msg_type: '<package/MsgType>'
        frequency_hz: <number|null>
        frequency_source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
        qos_profile: '<if specified>'
    subscribes:
      - topic: '<topic_name>'
        msg_type: '<package/MsgType>'
        callback_file: '<path>'
        callback_lines: '<range>'
    services:
      - name: '<service_name>'
        srv_type: '<package/SrvType>'
        role: <server|client>
        source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
    action_servers:
      - name: '<action_name>'
        action_type: '<package/ActionType>'
        role: <server|client>
        supports_preemption: <true|false|unknown>
        source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
    parameters:
      - name: '<param_name>'
        type: '<data_type>'
        default: '<value>'
        dynamic_reconfigure: <true|false>
        source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
tf_tree:
  frames:
    - parent: '<frame_id>'
      child: '<frame_id>'
      publisher_node: '<node_name>'
      static: <true|false>
launch_composition:
  - launch_file: '<path>'
    nodes_launched: ['<node_name>', ...]
    includes: ['<other launch file>', ...]
    conditionals: ['<brief description of conditional logic>']
pipeline_chains:
  - name: '<descriptive name, e.g. perception_to_control>'
    chain: ['<node>→<topic>→<node>→<topic>→<node>']
    latency_budget_ms: <number|null>
    latency_source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>'}
    taxonomy_refs_involved: ['<T{n}-L{n}:skill_name>', ...]
    evidence_unit_refs: ['<E{nn}>', ...]
```
After the YAML block, add: '## System Architecture Narrative' (2-3 paragraphs describing the overall data flow) and '## Concurrency & Real-Time Design' (threading model, executor type, callback groups, priority settings — with file citations).

PIPELINE CHAINS ARE CRITICAL: The pipeline_chains section captures end-to-end data flow paths through the system. Stage 3 uses these to compose 'system-level' resume bullets that demonstrate architectural ownership. Each chain should trace the full path from sensor input to actuator output (or the relevant subset). The taxonomy_refs_involved field enables Stage 3 to generate cross-track bullets like 'Designed a perception-to-control pipeline spanning [list of skills]'. For non-ROS repos, adapt the chain concept to whatever IPC mechanism exists (HTTP call chains, message queue flows, shared-memory pipelines).
